generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, constants) visible in the AADL model or defined within the current AGREE annex.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) use correct AGREE scalar types: int, real, or bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define boolean expressions for range checks.
Assignments (`assign`) are for implementation-level bindings to existing outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by modifying the AADL model.
